mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window, or forward 20-day returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.10) when FEDFUNDS declines.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 4.0 percent, labor market slack signals impending corporate earnings deterioration, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns remain positive in 65 percent of instances where UNRATE exceeds 4.0 percent, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.01) during high-unemployment periods.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in retail sales and housing starts indicate collapsing consumer demand, which forces corporate revenue downgrades and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint declines in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict negative 60-day returns, with a walk-forward Sharpe ratio below 0.15 and Rank IC consistently negative.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a post-shock recovery phase reflects rapid capacity utilization and supply chain normalization, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases during post-shock recovery periods yield negative 5-day forward returns in over 55 percent of cases, or the mechanism shows zero predictive accuracy (Rank IC = 0.00) in this regime.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 290, persistent inflation forces aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity multiples and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns remain positive in 70 percent of observations when CPIAUCSL exceeds 290, or the threshold effect vanishes with a p-value above 0.05 in a regime-switching model.

mechanism_narrative: Strong nonfarm payroll growth combined with stable unemployment indicates robust labor demand without wage-price spirals, supporting corporate margins and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint PAYEMS-UNRATE signal yields a negative Rank IC over rolling 2-year windows, or positive forward returns occur in less than 40 percent of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding real gross domestic product reflects sustained aggregate demand growth, which directly translates into higher corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth fails to predict positive 60-day returns, with a walk-forward correlation coefficient below 0.05 and no statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a late-cycle expansion signals supply bottlenecks and inventory drawdowns, triggering profit warnings and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines in late-cycle expansions produce positive 5-day forward returns in over 60 percent of cases, or the signal's predictive accuracy drops below random chance (Rank IC < -0.02).
